question_title: Will Keiko Fujimori advance to the runoff in the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
summary: Keiko Fujimori is consistently polling in 2nd place (8.0% in the latest CPI poll from March 7, 2026) behind López Aliaga (12.7%) and ahead of a fragmented pack of challengers. Her 2.4-point lead over 3rd-place López Chau, combined with extreme field fragmentation (36 candidates), a dedicated Fujimorist base, and a 3-for-3 runoff track record (2011, 2016, 2021) make her very likely to advance. The main risk is the large undecided/blank vote (~37%) consolidating behind one challenger, but this is difficult in a 36-candidate field. Her corruption case was dismissed in January 2026, removing disqualification risk.

factors.description: Keiko consistently polls 2nd across all pollsters (Jan-Mar 2026), ranging 6.6%-10.0% with 8.0% in latest CPI poll (March 7)
Track record: made the runoff in all 3 prior elections (2011, 2016, 2021) - dedicated Fujimorist base provides a reliable floor of support
Extreme field fragmentation (36 candidates) makes it very hard for any single challenger to consolidate enough anti-Fujimori vote to overtake her 2nd-place position
Legal obstacle cleared: Cocktails corruption case dismissed Jan 2026, no disqualification risk
Gap to 3rd place is only 2.4 points (8.0% vs 5.6%) with ~37% undecided/blank voters - meaningful risk of being overtaken if undecided voters consolidate
Very high negative image (77.4% per CELAG) limits her upside and makes her vulnerable if anti-Fujimori sentiment coalesces around a single alternative

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.80 (80% probability Keiko Fujimori advances to the runoff)**

**Key evidence:**

- **Consistent 2nd place polling**: Every poll from Jan-Mar 2026 shows Keiko in 2nd with 6.6-10.0% (latest: 8.0% on March 7)
- **3-for-3 runoff record**: She advanced in 2011, 2016, and 2021, each time from a similar polling position
- **36-candidate fragmentation**: With this many candidates splitting the vote, her dedicated Fujimorist base (~7-10%) is very hard to displace
- **No legal barriers**: Her corruption case was dismissed in January 2026

**Main risk**: The 2.4-point gap to 3rd place (López Chau at 5.6%) is not insurmountable given ~37% undecided/blank voters. However, in such a fragmented field, consolidation around a single challenger would be exceptionally difficult.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as Yes if Keiko Fujimori advances to the runoff in the [2026 Peruvian presidential election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Peruvian_general_election).
fine_print:
This question will also resolve as Yes if Keiko Fujimori wins the 2026 Peruvian presidential election in the first round.

In the case of a dispute, this question will resolve based upon the determination of the National Jury of Elections.
